page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, utilizing a configuration of one thread per core under a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 10.67 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is categorized in the bottom 10%, and single-threaded gzip compression is average. However, the multi-core capabilities are more robust, with the PassMark CPU score, extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests all ranking in the top 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also shows strong performance in the top 25%, while memory bandwidth and PassMark database operations remain in the middle 50% tier.

The shared CPU allocation model presents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated single-core performance. Qualitatively, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of over 10 GB per core offers resource density for memory-intensive applications without the cost of dedicated CPU cores. This configuration is suited for large-scale multi-threaded processing, memory-bound database operations, and caching layers, while it is less suited for tasks dependent on high single-core clock speeds or local storage.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 48 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system features 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding 10.67 GB of memory per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between poor single-core execution and strong multi-core capabilities, with PassMark CPU and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores placing in the top 25%. Conversely, memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. This resource profile is suited for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads such as caching systems and database operations, though the shared CPU allocation and lower single-core performance present tradeoffs for compute-bound applications.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-512GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and shared CPU allocation, delivering strong multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring forty-eight AMD EPYC cores and half a terabyte of RAM for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and physical cores on x86_64 architecture
512.0 GB of system memory providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core
Strong multi-core performance ranking in the top 25% of PassMark benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation on KVM hypervisor with no local storage or GPU
Suited for memory-bound databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded processing
